stint_ids: Grantham, Alexander William George Herder___Hong Kong___1948
persons.arguments_against: The only conceivable counter-hypothesis is a same-name relative or namesake as Governor of Hong Kong. It is not viable: the staff-list name reproduces the full, highly distinctive four-forename string 'Alexander William George Herder' exactly; the Governorship of Hong Kong is a unique office; the biography itself states 'gov. and c.-in-c., H.K., 1947', one year before the stint's first listing; the stint's K.C.M.G. matches the biography's K.C.M.G. (1945); and the career shape closes the loop — the biography starts him as 'cadet, Hong-Kong Nov., 1922', making a return as Governor the natural terminal post. The G.C.M.G. appearing from the 1952 list is a later honour the (older) printed biography versions simply had not yet recorded, which is the normal pattern, not a discrepancy. The position_sim score of 59 vs bar 60 is a one-point abbreviation artifact ('gov. and c.-in-c.' vs 'Governor and Commander-in-Chief').

persons.would_overturn: Evidence that a second Alexander William George Herder Grantham existed and governed Hong Kong in 1948-57, contradicting the biography's own 1947 appointment line.

notes: Full four-forename identity, unique gubernatorial office matching the biography's 1947 appointment, agreeing honour state, and 5 strict edition co-occurrences. The honour upgrade to G.C.M.G. in 1952 post-dates the last bio version's honour list, as expected.
